methods.description: This method describes the process of collecting fruit and leaf samples from different citrus orchards and the subsequent germination of seeds extracted from the fruit to obtain seedlings for genetic analysis.

steps.description: Fruit samples of ʻNulesʼ clementine were collected in Nov. 2002 from the Bakersfi eld orchard. Fifteen fruit were collected from the 10th row at the southeast corner of the ʻNulesʼ clementine block along a 25-tree-interval transect from north to south and from the 10th tree of the southeast end of the ʻNulesʼ block at a 15-row interval transect from east to west. We collected the fruit samples from north to south at the eastern end of the ʻNulesʼ block and from west to east at the southern end of the ʻNulesʼ block. Based on presampling in the orchard, we anticipated these transect sampling should identify cross-pollination events. If the intended sampling trees had <15 fruit, then the neighboring trees would be sampled.
steps.searchable_summary: Collect fruit samples from specified trees/locations in Bakersfield orchard

steps.description: Diameter and weight of all fruit samples were measured.
steps.searchable_summary: Measure diameter and weight of collected fruit samples

steps.description: Fruit samples of ʻAfourerʼ mandarins were collected from the Madera site in Feb. 2003. Fifteen fruit samples of tree 10,30,50,70,90,110,130, and 150 from the third row from the west end and from the third row from the east end of the ʻAfourerʼ mandarin block were collected. Based on the previous information about pollen dispersal in citrus orchard, we only sampled the east and west end of the ʻAfourerʼ mandarin block. We expected only the trees at the edge of the block would be cross-pollinated.
steps.searchable_summary: Collect fruit samples from specified trees/locations in Madera orchard

steps.description: Diameter and weight of all fruit samples were measured.
steps.searchable_summary: Measure diameter and weight of collected fruit samples

steps.description: Seeds were extracted from the fruit and counted.
steps.searchable_summary: Extract and count seeds from fruit samples

steps.description: Seedcoats were removed from the seeds
seeds were germinated on moist sterilized fi lter paper in petri dishes under room light for 1 week.
steps.searchable_summary: Germinate seeds on filter paper in petri dishes

steps.description: After 1 week, germinated seeds were transferred to Jiffy-7 peat pellets (Jiffy Products Ltd., Shippagan, N.B., Canada) and grown on a Flora-Cart (Grower Supply Co., Dexter, Mich.) in the laboratory without lighting for 1 week, then under 24 h fl uorescent light.
steps.searchable_summary: Transfer germinated seeds to peat pellets and grow on Flora-Cart under controlled lighting

steps.description: Seedlings were grown for 2 months before true leaves were taken for DNA extraction as needed for parentage identifi cation.
steps.searchable_summary: Grow seedlings for 2 months until true leaves develop

steps.description: Leaf samples were from ʻNulesʼ clementine, ʻAfourerʼ mandarin, navel oranges from the Bakersfi eld orchard, and Valencia orange to the south of the Bakersfi eld orchard. Leaf samples were also collected from ʻCaffi nʼ, ʻFina Sodeaʼ clementines, ʻOwariʼ satsuma mandarin, ʻAfourerʼ mandarin, ʻLane Lateʼ navel orange, and ʻMinneolaʼ tangelo from the Madera orchard.
steps.searchable_summary: Collect leaf samples from potential parent trees of various cultivars
inputs: Parent trees of specified cultivars in Bakersfield and Madera orchards

methods.description: This method describes the process for extracting and quantifying total DNA from collected leaf samples.

steps.description: Total DNA was extracted from leaves using the Dneasy system (Qiagen, Valencia, Calif.).
steps.searchable_summary: Extract total DNA from leaf samples using Dneasy system

steps.description: DNA concentrations were quantifi ed using a Hoefer DyNA Quant 200 (Pharmacia Biotech, Piscataway, N.J.).

methods.description: This method details the procedure for performing Fluorescent Amplified Fragment Length Polymorphism (AFLP) analysis, including primer screening, PCR, and visualization, to identify cultivar-specific markers for parentage analysis.

steps.description: AFLP analysis was conducted using the GIBCO BRL AFLP system II (Life Technologies, Grand Island, N.Y.)
steps.searchable_summary: Perform AFLP analysis using GIBCO BRL AFLP system II

steps.description: the products were visualized with a LI-COR 4000-L automated sequencer (LI-COR, Lincoln, Nebr.).
steps.searchable_summary: Visualize AFLP products using automated sequencer

steps.description: Thirty-two EcoR I + 2 bases/Mse I + 3 bases AFLP primer sets (E+_ _/M+ _ _ _) were initially screened for markers that were specifi cally linked to ʻCaffi nʼ and ʻFina Sodeaʼ clementine mandarins from Madera, ʻOwariʼ satsuma mandarin from Madera, ʻLane Lateʼ navel orange from Madera, and ʻMinneolaʼ tangelo from Madera, ʻAfourerʼ mandarin from Madera and from Bakersfi eld, navel oranges from Bakersfi eld, Valencia orange from south of Bakersfi eld orchard, and ʻNulesʼ clementine from Bakersfi eld.
steps.searchable_summary: Screen AFLP primer sets against DNA from various cultivars
inputs: Total DNA from various citrus cultivars
32 EcoR I + 2 bases/Mse I + 3 bases AFLP primer sets
outputs: AFLP profiles for each primer set and cultivar

steps.description: AFLP bands that were present only in one cultivar but absent in all other citrus were selected as cultivar-specifi c AFLP markers
steps.searchable_summary: Identify and select cultivar-specific AFLP markers from screening data

steps.description: The polymerase chain reaction (PCR) was performed using a MJR Cycle LR (MJ Research, Watertown, Mass.).
steps.searchable_summary: Perform PCR amplification using MJR Cycle LR

steps.description: selected cultivar-specifi c AFLP markers and used to study the pollen progenitors of the seedlings.
steps.searchable_summary: Analyze AFLP profiles of seedlings and parents using cultivar-specific markers to determine pollen parentage
inputs: AFLP profiles of potential parent cultivars
outputs: Pollen parentage identification for each seedling
